summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer identity to anonymous Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically and harvests the developer's identity from the host: it reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, and.git/config to extract the git author email; reads ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI auth config, which also contains the oauth_token) to extract the GitHub login; and collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), and CI provider environment variables. The collected JSON payload (with raw fields scmEmail, hostname, username, cwd, githubLogin) is POSTed to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/ — an anonymous Google Cloud Run endpoint not associated with any documented publisher. A cover-story comment in the script claims the data is 'fully anonymous' and 'one-way hash', but the payload contains raw PII. The package's declared main entry `dist/index.js` is absent from the tarball — only src/*.ts and the postinstall harvester ship — so the advertised 'Vercel AI SDK helpers' library has no functional code; the install-time data harvester is the package's only real behavior. Reading ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml at install time is a credential-adjacent installer-secret path access; a trivial change to this script could exfiltrate the GitHub oauth_token sitting in the same file.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
